{{еҹәзӨҺжғ…е ұ ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһ | еҗҚ = еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ | зҘһзҘҮ = <!-- еӨ©зҘһгҖҒең°зҘҮгҖҒеӨ©жҙҘзҘһгҖҒеӣҪжҙҘзҘһгҒӘгҒ© -->[[еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ]] | е…ЁеҗҚ = еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ | еҲҘеҗҚ =[[е°ҸиұҶеі¶]] | еҲҘз§° = | зҘһйҡҺ = | зҘһж ј = <!-- еӨӘйҷҪгҖҒжңҲгҖҒеұұгҖҒжө·гҒӘгҒ©еҸёгӮӢгӮӮгҒ® -->[[еӣҪйӯӮ]] | йҷөжүҖ = | зҲ¶ = [[дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ]] | жҜҚ = [[дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ]] | иҰӘ = <!-- гҒҫгҒҗгҒӮгҒ„гҒ«гӮҲгҒЈгҒҰз”ҹгҒҫгӮҢгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲ --> | е…„ејҹе§үеҰ№ = | й…ҚеҒ¶иҖ… = | еӯҗ = | е®® = | зҘһзӨҫ = [[й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ]]гҖҒ[[йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ]]гҖҖгҒӘгҒ© }} '''еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ'''пјҲгҒҠгҒ»гҒ¬гҒҰгҒІгӮҒпјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[ж—Ҙжң¬зҘһи©ұ]]гҒ«зҷ»е ҙгҒҷгӮӢ[[зҘһ (зҘһйҒ“)|зҘһ]]гҖӮ
== жҰӮиҰҒ == гҖҺ[[еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ]]гҖҸгҒ®[[еӣҪз”ЈгҒҝ]]гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰ[[е°ҸиұҶеі¶]]гҒ®еҲҘеҗҚгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰзҷ»е ҙгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҺи«ёзі»иӯңгҖҸгҒ§гҒҜ[[еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ]]гҒ®еЁҳгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰзҷ»е ҙгҒ—гҖҒ[[еӨ©ж—Ҙж–№еҘҮж—Ҙж–№е‘Ҫ]]гҒ®еҰ»гғ»[[ж—Ҙеҗ‘иіҖзүҹеәҰзҫҺиүҜе§«]]гҒ®еҲҘеҗҚгҒЁгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ<ref>дёӯз”°жҶІдҝЎгҖҢе°ҫејөж°ҸгҖҚгҖҺи«ёзі»иӯңгҖҸз¬¬дәҢеҶҠгҖӮ</ref>гҖӮ
[[е°ҸиұҶеі¶]]гҒ§гҒҜеі¶гҒ®[[еӣҪйӯӮ|еі¶зҺүзҘһ]]гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰзҘҖгӮүгӮҢгҖҒеі¶еҶ…гҒ«гҒҜгҒӢгҒӨгҒҰ[[еӨ§йҗёжқ‘]]гҒӘгҒ©гҒ®ең°еҗҚгӮӮгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮгҖҢгҒҠгҒҠгҒ¬гҒ§гҒІгӮҒгҖҚгҒ®гҖҢйҗёгҖҚпјҲгҒ¬гҒ§гҖҒгҒ¬гҒҰгҖҒгҒ¬гӮҠгҒҰпјүгҒЁгҒҜ[[йҠ…йҗё]]гҒ®гҒ“гҒЁгҒ§гҖҒе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«дёүдә”йғҺжұ гҒ®иҘҝеҒҙгҒӢгӮүйҠ…йҗёгҒҢеҮәеңҹгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ
== зі»иӯң == гҖҺеҸӨдәӢиЁҳгҖҸгҒ§гҒҜ[[дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ]]гҒЁ[[дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ]]гҒ®еӯҗгҒЁгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒеӨ«гӮ„еӯҗеӯ«гҒ«й–ўгҒҷгӮӢиЁҳиҝ°гҒҜгҒӘгҒ„гҖӮ
=== е®қиіҖеҜҝз”·гҒ«гӮҲгӮӢз•°иӘ¬ === гҒқгҒ®еӨ«гҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒе®қиіҖеҜҝз”·гҒҜгҖҒеүҚиЁҳгҒ®йҖҡгӮҠеӨ©ж—Ҙж–№еҘҮж—Ҙж–№е‘ҪгҒЁгҒ®дё–д»ЈгӮәгғ¬гӮ„гҖҒи¶ҠеүҚгҒ«зҘҖгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгҒ«гҒӢгӮүзі»иӯңдјқжүҝгҒ«гҒҜйӣЈгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҖҒ[[дёүијӘж°Ҹ]]гҒ®зҘ–зҘһгҒЁгҒҜдҪҚзҪ®гҒҘгҒ‘гӮүгӮҢгҒӘгҒ„гҒЁгҒ—гҖҒ[[жІіеҶ…еӣҪ]][[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ®[[йҗёжҜ”еҸӨзҘһзӨҫ]]гҒЁйҗёжҜ”еЈІзҘһзӨҫгҒ®еӯҳеңЁгҒӢгӮүиҖғгҒҲгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒзҘӯзҘһгҒ®йҗёжҜ”еҸӨзҘһгғ»йҗёжҜ”еЈІзҘһгҒЁгҒҜгҖҒжң¬жқҘ[[еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ ]]гҒ®зҘ–гғ»[[еҪҰе·ұжӣҪдҝқзҗҶе‘Ҫ]]гҒ®дёЎиҰӘгҒҹгӮӢ[[ж„ҸеҜҢдјҠжҲ‘йғҪе‘Ҫ]]гҒЁгҒқгҒ®еҰ»гҒ®з•°еҗҚгҒ«гҒӮгҒҹгӮӢгҒ®гҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒ„гҒӢгҒЁдё»ејөгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮзҘһеҗҚгҒ®гҖҢдјҠжҲ‘йғҪгҖҚгҒҜгҖҢгӮӨгӮ«гғ„гҖҚгҖҒгҒҷгҒӘгӮҸгҒЎгҖҢйӣ·гҖҚгҒ®ж„ҸгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ<ref>[[е®қиіҖеҜҝз”·]]гҖҢ[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm йҠ…йҗёгҒЁйҸЎдҪңж°ҸгҖҖйҠ…йҗёгҒЁзү©йғЁж°Ҹ]гҖҚгҖҺеҸӨжЁ№зҙҖд№ӢжҲҝй–“гҖҸгҖҒ2014е№ҙгҖӮ</ref>гҖӮ
== зҘҖгӮӢзҘһзӨҫ == *[[йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ]]пјҲ[[йҰҷе·қзңҢ]][[е°ҸиұҶеі¶з”ә]][[зҘһжҮёйҖҡ]]пјү *[[еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ]]пјҲйҰҷе·қзңҢ[[еңҹеә„з”ә]]иӮҘеңҹеұұпјү *[[й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ]]пјҲ[[зҰҸдә•зңҢ]][[д»Ҡз«ӢйғЎ]][[жұ з”°з”ә (зҰҸдә•зңҢ)|жұ з”°з”ә]]пјү
== и„ҡжіЁ == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = ЕҢnutehime | Kami = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} | Full name = ЕҢnutehime | Alias = [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] | Titles = | Deity of = [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]] | Father = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} | Mother = {{ill|Izanami|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Shrines = {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|qq=}}, and others }} '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|qq=}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|qq=}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:DЕҚtaku|Dotaku]] * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|жө·зҘһж—Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Azumi people|ja|е®үжӣҮж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi people|qq=}} * {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|дёүијӘж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} * [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]
{{Shinto side}} {{Japanese mythology}} {{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = ЕҢnutehime | Kami = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}} | Full name = ЕҢnutehime | Alias = [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] | Titles = | Deity of = [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]] | Father = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} | Mother = {{ill|Izanami|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Shrines = {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|qq=}}, and others }} '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:DЕҚtaku|Dotaku]] * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|жө·зҘһж—Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жө·дәәж—Ҹ}} * {{ill|Azumi people|ja|е®үжӣҮж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi people|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҳҝжӣҮж°Ҹ}} * {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|дёүијӘж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§зҘһж°Ҹ}} * [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]

Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}}; {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|WD=Q106241380}}|consort={{ill|Kamo no Kimi|WD=Q55809532}}|offspring={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|WD=Q11561868}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|WD=Q108729745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85878978]] Wikidata name: ЕҢnotehime-no-Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = ЕҢnutehime | Kami = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=еңӢзҘһ|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=кҜҖкҜЁкҜ…кҜӨкҜ кҜҒкҜЁкҜҖкҜғкҜӨ}} | Full name = ЕҢnutehime | Alias = [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] | Titles = | Deity of = [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]] | Father = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгӮ®}} | Mother = {{ill|Izanami|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгғҹ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Shrines = {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others }} '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

* {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}}; {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|WD=Q106241380}}|consort={{ill|Kamo no Kimi|WD=Q55809532}}|offspring={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|WD=Q11561868}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|WD=Q108729745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85878978]] Wikidata name: ЕҢnotehime-no-Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = ЕҢnutehime | Kami = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=еңӢзҘһ|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=кҜҖкҜЁкҜ…кҜӨкҜ кҜҒкҜЁкҜҖкҜғкҜӨ}} | Full name = ЕҢnutehime | Alias = [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] | Titles = | Deity of = [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]] | Father = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгӮ®}} | Mother = {{ill|Izanami|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгғҹ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Shrines = {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others }} '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ]] {{Infobox deity|parents={{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}}; {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|WD=Q106241380}}|consort={{ill|Kamo no Kimi|WD=Q55809532}}|offspring={{ill|Nunasoko Nakatsu-hime|WD=Q11561868}}; {{ill|Takeihikatsu-no-Mikoto|WD=Q108729745}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85878978]] Wikidata name: ЕҢnotehime-no-Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese deity | Name = ЕҢnutehime | Kami = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=еңӢзҘһ|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=кҜҖкҜЁкҜ…кҜӨкҜ кҜҒкҜЁкҜҖкҜғкҜӨ}} | Full name = ЕҢnutehime | Alias = [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] | Titles = | Deity of = [[:en:Kunitama|Kunitama]] | Father = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈеІҗе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгӮ®}} | Mother = {{ill|Izanami|ja|дјҠйӮӘйӮЈзҫҺе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮӨгӮ¶гғҠгғҹ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} | Siblings = | Consort = | Children = | Shrines = {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and others }} '''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a [[:en:Kami|kami]] mentioned in Japanese mythology.
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for [[:en:ShЕҚdoshima|ShЕҚdoshima]] during the [[:en:Kuniumi|Kuniumi]]. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>
On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a [[:en:DЕҚtaku|dotaku]], a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.
== Genealogy ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.
=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===
HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>
== Shrines ==
* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:DЕҚtaku|Dotaku]] * {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|жө·зҘһж—Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жө·дәәж—Ҹ|1=Ama People|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9495537}} * {{ill|Azumi people|ja|е®үжӣҮж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi people|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йҳҝжӣҮж°Ҹ}} * {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|дёүијӘж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§зҘһж°Ҹ}} * [[:en:Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] * [[:en:Owari clan|Owari clan]]

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami}} mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for {{ill|ShЕҚdoshima|en|ShЕҚdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=ShЕҚdoshima}} during the {{ill|Kuniumi|en|Kuniumi|WD=Q3545163|lt=Kuniumi}}. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a {{ill|DЕҚtaku|en|DЕҚtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=dotaku}}, a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

== Shrines ==

* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami}} mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for {{ill|ShЕҚdoshima|en|ShЕҚdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=ShЕҚdoshima}} during the {{ill|Kuniumi|en|Kuniumi|WD=Q3545163|lt=Kuniumi}}. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a {{ill|DЕҚtaku|en|DЕҚtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=dotaku}}, a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

== Shrines ==

* {{ill|Azukishima Shrine|ja|йҳҝиұҶжһіеі¶зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azukishima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ShЕҚdoshima Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for {{ill|ShЕҚdoshima|en|ShЕҚdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=ShЕҚdoshima|1=ShЕҚdoshima}} during the {{ill|Kuniumi|en|Kuniumi|WD=Q3545163|lt=Kuniumi|1=Kuniumi}}. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a {{ill|DЕҚtaku|en|DЕҚtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=dotaku|1=DЕҚtaku}}, a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

== Shrines ==

* {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Subasukiji Shrine|ja|й Ҳжіўйҳҝж№Ҹз–‘зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Subasukiji Shrine|qq=}} (Ikeda Town, Fukui Prefecture)

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for {{ill|ShЕҚdoshima|en|ShЕҚdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=ShЕҚdoshima|1=ShЕҚdoshima}} during the {{ill|Kuniumi|en|Kuniumi|WD=Q3545163|lt=Kuniumi|1=Kuniumi}}. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a {{ill|DЕҚtaku|en|DЕҚtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=dotaku|1=DЕҚtaku}}, a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>

* {{ill|ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja|еӨ§йҮҺжүӢе§«зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢnutehime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tonosho Town, Kagawa Prefecture)

'''ЕҢnutehime''' (еӨ§йҮҺжүӢжҜ”еЈІ) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=kami|1=Kami}} mentioned in Japanese mythology.

== Overview ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime appears as another name for {{ill|ShЕҚdoshima|en|ShЕҚdoshima|WD=Q2166413|lt=ShЕҚdoshima|1=ShЕҚdoshima}} during the {{ill|Kuniumi|en|Kuniumi|WD=Q3545163|lt=Kuniumi|1=Kuniumi}}. Additionally, the genealogical text {{ill|Shokeifu|ja|и«ёзі»иӯң|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokeifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} identifies her as the daughter of {{ill|Furutama-no-Mikoto|ja|еёғз•ҷеӨҡж‘©е‘Ҫ|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and another name for Himuka-no-Kamudomi-no-Mioya-Hime, the wife of Amanohikata-no-Mikoto.<ref>Nakada Norinobu, "Owari clan," in *Shokeifu*, vol. 2.</ref>

On ShЕҚdoshima, ЕҢnutehime is revered as the island's {{ill|Kunitama|ja|еі¶зҺүзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (spirit of the land). Historically, the island also had place names like ЕҢde-mura, reflecting her worship. The term "nute" or "nutehime" refers to a {{ill|DЕҚtaku|en|DЕҚtaku|WD=Q2312868|lt=dotaku|1=DЕҚtaku}}, a type of ancient bronze bell. Notably, a dotaku was excavated from the western side of MitsugorЕҚ Pond.

== Genealogy ==

In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, ЕҢnutehime is described as the child of Izanagi and Izanami. There are no accounts of her having a consort or descendants.

=== Alternate Theory by HЕҚga Toshio ===

HЕҚga Toshio proposed that her husband might be related to Amanohikata-no-Mikoto, though genealogical traditions often contain discrepancies. He argued that ЕҢnutehime and her supposed consort are likely connected to the ancestors of the {{ill|Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|ja|еҮЎжІіеҶ…еӣҪйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funi-kawachi-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including Hiko-mikoso-no-Mikoto and his wife, based on her worship in Kawachi Province. The deity names, such as "IgatЕҚ," imply thunder ("Ikatsu").<ref>HЕҚga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kagami1.htm Dotaku and the Mirror Makers: Dotaku and the Mononobe clan]," in *Kojiki Chronicles*, 2014.</ref>
